title: Detox Pathway
bodyPreview: Unlock Your Detox Potential with a DNA Test! This Detox Pathway report analyzes your genetic makeup to provide personalized insights into how your body processes and eliminates toxins. By understanding your unique genetic blueprint, you can optimize your detox pathways to reduce inflammation, oxidative stress, and chronic illness. Phase I Detox Phase I detoxification is the first step in your body’s process of breaking down harmful substances. Enzymes encoded by genes like CYP1A2, CYP3A4, CYP2C9, and CYP2C19 convert toxins and drugs into intermediate compounds. While essential, this step can create reactive byproducts, increasing oxidative stress if not managed effectively in Phase II. Genetic variations in these genes influence how your body processes substances, affecting drug metabolism and toxin clearance. Phase II Detox Phase II detoxification neutralizes reactive intermediates from Phase I and prepares them for elimination. Genes like NAT2 and COMT regulate key pathways, while antioxidants like glutathione provide additional protection. Variations in these genes can impact your ability to handle oxidative stress and detoxify efficiently. Insights into your Phase II detox capacity can guide strategies to optimize your body’s natural defenses through diet, lifestyle, and supplements.
